scenario: Evergreen Insurance's Camunda 8.8 invoice flow includes an ArchiveInvoiceWorker that translates a specific business exception from its ArchiveService into a BPMN error. The team handles 1800 invoices per day and is reviewing which collaborator should be mocked when authoring the process test for this worker.
question: Which collaborator should the Evergreen team mock in the process test for ArchiveInvoiceWorker?

options.text: Mock the ArchiveService called by the worker, keep the ArchiveInvoiceWorker running as the production class so input mapping and business-exception-to-BPMN-error translation are still exercised end to end
Mock the ArchiveInvoiceWorker itself with a stub that completes the job successfully, so the test focuses on the BPMN model and does not depend on the worker class at all
Mock the Zeebe client used by the ArchiveInvoiceWorker to short-circuit job activation, so neither the worker code nor the ArchiveService is reached during the process test
Mock both the ArchiveInvoiceWorker and the ArchiveService at the same time, then assert on the FEEL expressions executed by the engine in the process tests run by CI

a.text: Correct. The Test scope and mocking guidance keeps the worker class in the test so its input data mapping and business-exception-to-BPMN-error translation are validated, and mocks only the engine-independent ArchiveService so the test never calls the real legacy system.

b.text: Incorrect. Mocking the ArchiveInvoiceWorker removes the very glue code the process test is supposed to cover, including the input mapping and the BPMN error translation.

c.text: Incorrect. Mocking the Zeebe client takes the engine out of the picture entirely, which defeats the purpose of a process test that drives the BPMN model.

d.text: Incorrect. Mocking both the worker and the service skips the same glue code the process test is meant to verify and leaves nothing meaningful to assert.

explanation: In a Camunda 8.8 process test the worker class is executed for real so its input mapping and BPMN error translation are covered, while only the workflow engine-independent backend service it calls is replaced by a mock.
